tools.name: scan_repository
tools.description: Submit a GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository for post-quantum cryptography (PQC) vulnerability scanning. Returns a scan_id. Call get_scan_result to poll for the result. Free tier: 10 scans/day per IP. Paid tier: credit-based (register at /api/agent/register).

tools.name: get_scan_result
tools.description: Get the result of a previously submitted scan. Returns status (submitted | working | completed | failed) and, when completed, a full CBOM (Cryptographic Bill of Materials) compliant with EIP-7789 and CycloneDX CBOM 1.6, plus a quantum risk score (0 = fully safe, 100 = fully vulnerable). Rate-limited

tools.name: check_pqc_risk
tools.description: Instant check (no DB, no scan) — returns whether a list of algorithm names are quantum-vulnerable. Useful for quick risk assessment before calling scan_repository. Rate-limited: 100 calls/day per IP (free), 500/day per API key (paid).

tools.name: scan_contract
tools.description: Scan a verified on-chain smart contract for quantum-vulnerable cryptography AND today's fraud patterns (rug pulls, honeypots, uncapped mints, reentrancy). Use this BEFORE signing a transaction, interacting with a DeFi protocol, or integrating a contract into an agent workflow. Synchronous — result i

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "check_pqc_risk" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Instant check (no DB, no scan) — returns whether a list of algorithm names are quantum-vulnerable. Useful for quick risk assessment before calling scan_reposito…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
